page: Amazon Web Services r6a.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 vCPUs derived from 96 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r6a.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores, which rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory subsystem performance is also highly competitive, with small-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching top-tier status, while large-block read and write bandwidths register in the top 25%. Database operations, such as Redis SET commands, achieve top-tier throughput at 176.67 million operations per second, and PassMark database operations score in the top 25%. Single-core performance and text processing tasks, however, align with average middle-tier benchmarks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6a.48xlarge offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads, balancing a massive RAM footprint with substantial multi-threaded compute capacity. The lack of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, which is advantageous for architectures utilizing external storage arrays but represents a tradeoff for IOPS-heavy local database requirements. This server is suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database deployments, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r6a.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-to-vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU hardware, but provides a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the r6a.48xlarge achieves top-tier results in multi-core CPU performance, small-block memory bandwidth, and Redis SET operations, while single-core metrics remain in the average tier. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, and in-memory caching applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR4 RAM, and AMD EPYC 7R13 processors, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring 192 vCPUs and 1.5 TB of RAM for scale-out database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores via AMD EPYC 7R13 processors
1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory with top-tier small-block read bandwidth
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and Redis SET benchmark performance
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and large-scale data processing
